MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sarah Marsey, Esq. (415-281-7604) for Defendant/Moving party.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT General Media Communications, Inc's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication against Plaintiffs Carlos Garay and Sergio Garay.

Defendant General Media Communications, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. In his opposition papers, plaintiff Carlos Garay abandoned his second, third and fourth causes of action for negligent hiring, negligent retention, and premises liability against GMCI. As to the first cause of action for negligence, GMCI presented sufficient evidence showing that it owed no duty to Carlos Garay to prevent or mitigate the violence committed against Mr. Garay. Mr. Garay did not present any evidence creating a triable issue regarding duty. The undisputed facts establish that GMCI did not participate in any of the events that led to the violence committed against Mr. Garay and that GMCI is not a party to any agreement which bestows a benefit on a class of persons including Mr. Garay. The licensing agreement did not create a duty on the part of GMCI. That agreement was not entered into for the benefit of third parties like Mr. Garay. Johnson v. Holmes Tuttle Lincoln-Mercury (1958) 160 Cal.App.2d 290, 300, relied on by Mr. Garay, is inapposite because unlike the agreement in Johnson, nothing in the licensing agreement shows any intent to benefit third persons. Moreover, GMCI never adopted, authorized, or enforced any workplace policies for BT California, LLC's employees. Because there is no liability on the first cause of action, the seventh cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress alleged by Sergio Garay, which is entirely dependent on the first cause of action, also fails. Plaintiffs' objections to the licensing agreement are overruled. Joseph Carouba's declaration properly authenticates that agreement.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sarah Marsey, Esq. (415-281-7604) for Defendant/Moving party.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOSEPH CAROUBA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication against Plaintiffs Carlos Garay and Sergio Garay.

Defendant Joseph Carouba's motion for summary judgment is granted. In his opposition papers, plaintiff Carlos Garay abandoned his second and third causes of action for negligent hiring and negligent retention against Mr. Carouba. As to the first cause of action for negligence and the fourth cause of action for premises liability, Mr. Carouba presented sufficient evidence showing that he owed no duty to Carlos Garay to prevent or mitigate the violence committed against Mr. Garay and is not liable as an alter ego of either BT California LLC or 408-412 Broadway, LLC. Mr. Garay did not present any evidence creating a triable issue regarding either duty or alter ego liability. The undisputed facts establish that Mr. Carouba did not participate in any of the events that led to the violence committed against Mr. Garay and that Mr. Carouba is not a party to any agreement which bestows a benefit on a class of persons including Mr. Garay. The undisputed facts also show that there is not a unity of interest between Mr. Carouba and the two LLCs to support imposition of alter ego liability against Mr. Carouba. Neither the lease for the Penthouse Club nor the licensing agreement created a duty on the part of Mr. Carouba, who was not party to either agreement. Neither agreement was entered into for the benefit of third parties like Mr. Garay. Even assuming that Mr. Carouba failed to delegate authority, that alone is not a basis to impose liability on Mr. Carouba. Because there is no liability on the first and fourth causes of action, the seventh cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress alleged by Sergio Garay, which is entirely dependent on the first and fourth causes of action, also fails. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Carouba's declaration are overruled. Mr. Carouba provides sufficient facts establishing his personal knowledge.
